subject: recovering theater kids
one_liner: Global payroll and compliance for people who can still do the whole monologue but have never once filed a W-2.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan HR-tech sincerity colliding with theater-kid grief. A corporate payroll job is framed as the role they finally booked. Treats steady direct deposit as the standing ovation that never came. Never winks at the audience.

hero.h1: You were going to be huge. Now you're on payroll.
hero.sub: Global payroll, contracts, and compliance for recovering theater kids. We handle the parts of adulthood that don't come with an intermission.

features.title: Misclassification, Resolved
features.blurb: The IRS wants to know whether you're an employee or a contractor. You want to know whether you're a leading type or a character actor. We answer the first question in writing and quietly table the second.

features.title: Employer of Record in 150 Countries
features.blurb: We become your legal employer anywhere you flee to after the cast party. Local payroll, local taxes, local benefits, so geography stops being your exit strategy.

features.title: Onboarding That Ships a Chair
features.blurb: A laptop, a standing desk, and one ergonomic chair arrive in five business days. We do not ship the spotlight. We have been very clear with everyone about the spotlight.

pricing_tiers.name: Open Call
perks: A welcome email you will leave unread

pricing_tiers.name: Callback
perks: Benefits that vest before you quit

pricing_tiers.name: Standing Ovation
pricing_tiers.tagline: For people who never really left.
perks: Recorded applause on every payroll run
Permission to say you work in operations now

testimonials.quote: I haven't been on a stage since 2014, and Residuals still pays me on the 1st. Longest run of my career.
testimonials.name: Dorian Ashby-Vale
testimonials.title: Former Mercutio, Senior Manager of Vendor Operations

testimonials.quote: My contractor agreement arrived formatted like a script. I gave it a cold read in the kitchen. It held.
testimonials.name: Priya Nandakumar
testimonials.title: Recovering Triple Threat, Revenue Enablement Lead

testimonials.quote: The applause meter is technically a push notification. My therapist and I are working through it.
testimonials.name: Wes Kowalczyk
testimonials.title: Ensemble 1998 to 2009, Director of Procurement 2019 to present

demo_screen_concept: A global payroll and compliance dashboard styled as a backstage call board. A left rail lists the user's legal entities across six countries like a cast list, each with a deadpan pay status. Tabs across the top switch between Payroll, Contracts, Compliance, and Green Room. The Payroll tab centers on a large Run Payroll button that, when pressed, fills an applause meter from polite clapping up to standing ovation and stamps each cast member PAID. The Contracts tab opens an international agreement in a modal formatted like a stage script, complete with stage directions. The Compliance tab is a pre-show checklist of tax and visa items rendered as toggles. Vanilla JS only: tab switching, the modal open and close, the animated applause meter, and the checklist toggles.

design_direction.style: Golden-age Broadway theater-program, dark mode. A deep curtain-aubergine ground carries a brass Art Deco vocabulary: stepped chevron borders, sunburst rules, a marquee-bulb divider motif, and thin gilt hairlines framing each section like a proscenium arch. An oversized Poiret One wordmark and headlines do the heavy lifting in geometric Deco caps; Didact Gothic carries calm body copy. One brass gold carries brand, ornament, links, and figures; one rationed curtain-red marks the single primary CTA and the now-playing tags and appears almost nowhere else. Every figure that pretends to be box-office data (currencies, pay dates, tier prices, run counts) is set in letter-spaced Didact Gothic caps. No gradients beyond a single soft spotlight radial behind the hero plate, no glassmorphism, no neon, no drop-shadow marketing cards. Generous 8px-rhythm spacing, hairline gilt rules between sections. Reads like a 1929 Playbill for a payroll platform that has been pointed, with total sincerity, at someone who peaked in the spring musical.
design_direction.archetype: Premium editorial landing rendered as a theater program: a marquee hero with an oversized Deco wordmark and a framed spotlit stage plate, an intro now-playing statement, three feature acts separated by gilt sunburst rules, three pull-quote testimonials with framed cast headshots, a three-tier house pricing rack styled as ticket classes, and a demo handoff to the backstage call-board console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Slow, curtain-like reveals (350-450ms ease-out), a gentle marquee-bulb shimmer on the section dividers, a subtle 1.02 scale on the CTA press. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Split marquee hero: oversized Poiret One wordmark and h1 flush left beneath a thin gilt proscenium rule, a framed spotlit empty-stage plate to the right with corner Deco crop-marks, the single curtain-red CTA below the subhead, and a letter-spaced NOW PLAYING kicker above the headline.

images.rationale: Theater-program style genuinely earns one photographic plate: a spotlit empty stage anchors the marquee hero better than pure type. Three deadpan corporate headshots of recovering theater kids replace the testimonial initials-circles. The demo is a call-board dashboard (terminal/document-shaped), so it gets no assets.

items.prompt: Editorial photograph of an empty golden-age theater stage from the dark house; single warm spotlight pool on bare boards; deep aubergine-burgundy velvet curtains and tarnished brass Art Deco proscenium; 1929 Broadway mood; aubergine #1E1015 with brass-gold #C8A24B highlights; no people, no text.
items.judge: PASS — empty spotlit stage, brass Deco proscenium, on-palette aubergine+gold, no people, no text artifacts.

items.prompt: Corporate headshot of a man in his late thirties, dark blazer and tie, deadpan composed expression, former-stage-actor posture, burgundy backdrop with brass-gold rim light, aubergine #1E1015 / gold #C8A24B; no text.
items.judge: PASS — correct subject, palette harmonizes, no text artifacts.

items.prompt: Corporate headshot of a woman in her thirties, charcoal blouse, suppressed knowing half-smile, poised former-triple-threat bearing, burgundy backdrop with brass-gold rim light; no text.
items.judge: PASS — correct subject, palette harmonizes, no text artifacts.
